sections.title: Front Matter
sections.body_text:
a Percentage calculated relative to the total.   
b Percentage calculated relative to the reactive proportion.

a All energies are shown in $\mathrm { k c a l \ m o l ^ { - 1 } }$ . Experimental activation free energies were obtained from $k _ { \mathrm { c a t } }$ values presented in Table S1, using transition state theory. Calculated values are average values and standard deviations over 30 independent empirical valence bond (EVB) simulations per system, as described in the Material and Methods section. “in” and “out” substrate conformations denote energies obtained from simulations initiated from the substrate starting in the corresponding in/out conformation. A two-sample Wilcoxon rank-sum test (two-sided) indicated statistically significant differences in the calculated activation free energies between the “in” and “out” conformations for Des27.7 $p$ -value $= 7 . 7 ^ { * } 1 0 ^ { - 9 }$ ) but not for Des27 $\dot { p }$ -value $= 0 . 0 8 8$ ).

Supplementary Table 4. Idealized geometries and allowed deviations for the catalytic constellation.

Supplementary Table 6. Non-standard force field parameters used to describe the substrate 5-nitrobenzisoxazole in our conventional molecular dynamics simulations.a

a All parameters were obtained using the General AMBER Force Field 2 (GAFF2)66, as outlined in the Experimental Section in the main text.

Supplementary Table 7. List of neutralized residues and histidine protonation patterns used in our EVB simulations of Des27 and Des27.7 variants, in complex with substrates 5-nitrobenzisoxazole. a

a Shown here are the residues that fall outside the explicit simulation sphere and were thus kept in their neutral form to avoid system instabilities created by having charged residues outside the water droplet (this is standard practice for such simulations). All other residues were kept in their usual ionization state at physiological $\mathsf { p H }$ . In the case of the histidine side chains, His-ε and His-δ indicate histidine side chains protonated at the $\mathrm { N } _ { \mathrm { { \varepsilon } } 2 }$ and $\mathrm { N } _ { \delta 1 }$ nitrogen atoms, respectively.

figures.caption: Supplementary Figure 1. Protein expression of designs from ablation study of Des27.7 (relative to main Figure 3). From left to right: marker, modular assembly, PROSS, modular assembly with active site, Des27.7 Asp162Met, Des27.7 Asp162Ala, Des27.7 Phe113Leu, Des27.7 without core stabilization. Purifications were performed 2-5 times. For gel source data, see Supplementary Data 1.
